summary: Closed, unmerged directory-split PR intended to isolate matching improvements for `src/melee/cm`, specifically `src/melee/cm/camera.c`, from a larger branch. The available dump contains no full diff or changed-file list, but the PR body reports it was a pathspec slice from a larger matching-improvement branch. Review feedback flagged a proposed replacement of `HSD_ASSERTMSG` with explicit `__assert` calls in `Camera_80029CF8` as unacceptable because it appeared aimed at matching data sections and was described as a regression despite improving data percentage.
what_changed: PR was split from #2617 by directory/subsystem to make matching improvements easier to review.
Declared scope was `src/melee/cm`; the only file named in the PR body was `src/melee/cm/camera.c`.

The preliminary report for the full unsplit source branch claimed +11944 matched-code bytes, +640 linked-code bytes, +448 matched-data bytes, 26 new matches, 79 improvements in unmatched items, and no broken matches/regressions before the slice-specific CI result.

A reviewed hunk in `Camera_80029CF8` changed two `HSD_ASSERTMSG` calls for `fov_r` and `fov_l` bounds into explicit `if (!(...)) { __assert(...); }` calls using `cm_803BCBD0`, `cm_803BCC14`, and `cm_803BCC30`.
The PR was closed without merge; the context reports zero changed files and an empty diff excerpt, so only the PR body and review hunk provide concrete change evidence.
smart_moves: Splitting a large matching-improvement branch by directory/subsystem was a reasonable reviewability tactic for decomp work.

The PR body recorded local checks and provenance: created from `origin/master`, isolated path slice, `git diff --check origin/master...HEAD` passed, and the unsplit source branch had green CI before splitting.
The PR explicitly deferred to per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting as the authoritative score for the slice rather than relying only on the full-branch report.
decomp_lessons: Do not make source changes with the intention of matching data sections; project contributing guidance treats that as invalid even if metrics such as matched-data percentage improve.
A data-percentage improvement can still be a regression when it worsens source quality, semantic clarity, or accepted decomp conventions.

Prefer preserving higher-level project macros such as `HSD_ASSERTMSG` when they express intended behavior, rather than manually lowering them to `__assert` solely to influence emitted data or string layout.
Directory-split matching PRs should still be scrutinized for local regressions; green status or better aggregate match numbers from an unsplit branch are not sufficient evidence of acceptability.
naming_conventions: `src/melee/cm` is the camera subsystem path/scope indicated by this PR.
Camera functions in this area use the `Camera_` prefix, e.g. `Camera_80029CF8`.
Camera-local data/string symbols in the reviewed hunk used the `cm_` prefix with address-like names, e.g. `cm_803BCBD0`, `cm_803BCC14`, `cm_803BCC30`.
Existing assertion style used `HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, condition, message)` rather than open-coded `__assert` in the reviewed code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Pathspec-based slicing from a larger matching branch was used to isolate subsystem changes.
The reviewed assertion rewrite suggests an attempted tactic of changing macro expansion/source form to affect data-section matching, but review explicitly rejected this as contrary to guidelines.
Local hygiene check `git diff --check origin/master...HEAD` was run for the slice.
review_feedback: ribbanya reviewed `src/melee/cm/camera.c` and stated that, per the AI-assistance/contributing guidelines, changes should not be made with the intention of matching data sections.
The same review comment called the highlighted `HSD_ASSERTMSG` to `__assert` change a regression even if it improved data percentage.
follow_up_queries: Find the originating PR #2617 to inspect the full unsplit matching branch and whether the CM changes were later corrected.
Search later PRs touching `src/melee/cm/camera.c` or `Camera_80029CF8` to see whether valid matching improvements landed without the rejected assertion/data-section tactic.
Check project contributing guidelines around AI assistance and data-section matching for the exact policy referenced by review.
